scope: Public-literature sanity check only for X-Calibur/XL-Calibur, 511-CAM, INTEGRAL/SPI, and COSI background composition, activation/delayed, prompt, anticoincidence/veto, line-window versus broad-band, and wide-field versus pointed focusing caveats.

sources.supports: XL-Calibur is a 15-80 keV balloon hard-X-ray polarimeter with CZT detector, anticoincidence shield, and stratospheric particle background mitigation.
XL-Calibur background-reduction design changes; residual background dominated by atmospheric neutrons and MeV gammas; 15-80 keV simulated background-rate table.
XL-Calibur BGO anticoincidence shield at about 40 km, veto threshold below 100 keV, measured 20-40 keV background about 0.5 Hz.
Prompt stratospheric background and activation/delayed gamma rays outside the anticoincidence time window.
BGO shield passive and active rejection, >99% charged-particle rejection, neutron leakage.
Flight 20-40 keV veto-off/on rates and <1 Hz goal.
511-CAM mass-scales XL-Calibur cosmic-ray and atmospheric background to >15 keV 60 Hz.
511-CAM MEGAlib simulation with realistic geometry, idealized mass model, and active BGO shield.
511-CAM 390 eV FWHM 511 keV peak, 93% interval 510.3-511.8 keV, single-pixel low-background selection and >99% X-Calibur background removal example.
511-CAM effective area, dominant balloon background modeling, shield leakage model, energy-resolution sensitivity scaling.
511-CAM is pointed and complements wide-field missions; diffuse case has low detection rate over low background.
SPI coded-mask Ge spectrometer, scintillation veto, cosmic-ray activation background, differing line/continuum behavior.
SPI data model is sky signal plus large instrumental background; background is not simply subtracted.
SPI instrumental continuum, nuclear lines, prompt/delayed radiation, anticoincidence veto, non-vetoed delayed/celestial triggers.
SPI 511 keV analysis uses instrumental background method and multi-component sky fitting.
COSI balloon 0.2-5 MeV wide-field high-resolution Compton telescope and 511 keV instrumental-background separation.
COSI intense 511 keV atmospheric line, activation lines, and need for precise background description.
COSI background simulation includes atmospheric and activation components; small balloon-environment variations influence 511 keV backgrounds.
COSI 46-day 0.2-5 MeV Compton telescope, strong background suppression possibility, empirical strong instrumental background, coded-mask caveat.
Soft gamma-ray instrumental background includes prompt and delayed activation; balloon 511 keV background is hard to model absolutely.
COSI background inferred empirically; expected instrumental background about 100 times 511 keV sky signal and dominates.
COSI pre-flight background estimates: 0.2-5 MeV Ge Compton telescope; prompt and delayed activation; low-energy extragalactic dominance and higher-energy delayed activation.
COSI Ge detectors surrounded by active BGO shields for attenuation and anticoincidence.
COSI continuum and activation-line composition; BGO veto suppresses prompt interactions and activation lines become prominent.
COSI summary: delayed activation non-negligible, especially for 511 keV.

instrument_type.value: Pointed balloon hard-X-ray polarimeter, 15-80 keV, Be scatterer plus CZT, active shield.

prompt_background.statement: Prompt stratospheric particle/gamma background is important after shielding; residual simulated background is mainly atmospheric neutrons and gamma rays.

activation_delayed.statement: Activation/delayed photons can evade anticoincidence, but X/XL-Calibur papers do not establish 511 keV delayed dominance for a TES-Laue focal plane.

anticoincidence_veto.statement: BGO veto can reduce prompt charged-particle background by orders of magnitude; flight veto-on rate is about 0.5 Hz in 20-40 keV.

line_window_vs_broad_band.statement: Published X/XL-Calibur rates are hard-X-ray 15-80 or 20-40 keV rates, not 511 keV narrow-line-window rates.

comparability_to_tes_laue.statement: Useful qualitative stratospheric/veto proxy, but absolute rates cannot be transplanted to a 511 keV TES-Laue selection.

instrument_type.value: Pointed 511 keV focusing gamma-ray telescope concept with stacked TES microcalorimeters and active BGO shield.

prompt_background.statement: 511-CAM treats the mission as background-limited and models balloon background components; literature does not imply delayed activation must dominate after final narrow-window TES selection.

activation_delayed.statement: 511-CAM's X/XL-Calibur transfer is a mass-scaled cosmic-ray/atmospheric >15 keV estimate, not a delayed-activation selected-rate validation.

anticoincidence_veto.statement: Active BGO shield, single-pixel selection, and optics-consistency checks are expected to suppress background.

line_window_vs_broad_band.statement: 390 eV FWHM and 510.3-511.8 keV reconstructed interval support treating narrow 511 keV line-window rates separately from broad-band rates.

comparability_to_tes_laue.statement: Closest public conceptual analog, but still simulation/concept with idealized mass model rather than flight-validated absolute background authority.

instrument_type.value: Space-based wide-field coded-mask Ge gamma-ray line spectrometer with scintillation veto.

prompt_background.statement: Prompt instrumental background is part of the SPI background model and cannot be separated by naive subtraction.

activation_delayed.statement: SPI literature strongly supports activation/delayed internal background and instrumental lines as central to 511 keV line analysis.

anticoincidence_veto.statement: SPI anticoincidence vetoes prompt charged-particle interactions, but non-vetoed events include delayed background or celestial triggers.

line_window_vs_broad_band.statement: SPI separates line and continuum components with empirical multi-component fitting; line and continuum behavior differ.

comparability_to_tes_laue.statement: Excellent evidence that activation can dominate line analysis, but coded-mask wide-field space Ge absolute rates are not transferable to a pointed balloon TES-Laue focal plane.

instrument_type.value: Balloon and planned satellite 0.2-5 MeV wide-field Ge Compton telescope with active shields.

prompt_background.statement: COSI literature supports strong prompt/instrumental and atmospheric background requiring empirical or semi-empirical treatment.

activation_delayed.statement: COSI literature supports activation/delayed line backgrounds and non-negligible 511 keV activation concerns.

anticoincidence_veto.statement: Active shields suppress prompt interactions, but this can leave activation lines prominent; veto does not remove activation modeling needs.

line_window_vs_broad_band.statement: COSI 511 keV analysis is a line-window and background-model problem; expected instrumental background is about 100 times the 511 keV sky signal.

comparability_to_tes_laue.statement: Strong sanity evidence for activation/background complexity, but wide-field Compton absolute rates are not direct proxies for pointed focusing TES-Laue final rates.

cross_instrument_judgments.statement: Prompt atmospheric/cosmic-ray background can be strongly reduced by anticoincidence and event topology in pointed balloon focal-plane instruments.
Activation/delayed internal backgrounds can be important, especially for Ge wide-field line instruments and material-rich configurations.
SPI and COSI absolute background fractions must not be directly applied to a pointed TES-Laue geometry.
X/XL-Calibur absolute 15-80 keV or 20-40 keV rates must not be directly applied to a 511 keV TES-Laue line-window final rate.
511-CAM literature supports treating narrow energy resolution and final event selection as decisive for final 511 keV composition.

Public literature does not make a small final delayed fraction in a pointed, shielded, narrow-window TES-Laue 511 keV selection physically impossible; it requires local normalization and selection audits.

what_literature_would_make_suspicious.condition: A close balloon-borne pointed 511 keV TES/Laue or channeling-optics payload with similar materials and veto timing shows flight-validated delayed/activation dominance after final narrow-window selection.
what_literature_would_make_suspicious.current_status: No such public source was found in this pass; the closest 511-CAM source is concept/simulation and uses a mass-scaled gross background estimate.

what_literature_would_make_suspicious.condition: The local delayed chain ignores delayed activation that can evade anticoincidence windows.
what_literature_would_make_suspicious.current_status: Literature says this would be suspicious; local audit must verify activity and decay normalization.

what_literature_would_make_suspicious.condition: Old delayed dominance appears in activated shield/detector materials or broad windows.
what_literature_would_make_suspicious.current_status: Literature makes that physically plausible, but does not prove old local normalization.

what_literature_would_make_suspicious.condition: Broad-band or wide-field background rates are compared directly to pointed narrow-line-window final rates.
what_literature_would_make_suspicious.current_status: Literature says this comparison is not valid without response, window, stage, and background-model alignment.

overall_verdict.statement: Public literature supports that delayed/activation dominance is expected for some Ge wide-field line instruments and material-rich configurations, while a pointed shielded TES-Laue narrow-window selection could plausibly have a much smaller final delayed component if local normalization and selection audits pass. Literature alone identifies neither a project BUG nor NO_BUG and rules out direct absolute-rate transfer from X/XL-Calibur, SPI, or COSI.
